etymology.description: 𣦃 (original) -> stylized as 前 (phonetic 歬 + semantic 刀) -> 前 replaced 歬; extra 刀 added -> final form 剪
etymology.interpretation: Initially represented by 𣦃 and later evolved by stylization into 前 with a phonetic component 歬 and a semantic component 刀 (a knife). To retain the semantic connection with cutting, an additional 刀 component was added after the phonetic part was replaced by 前.

examples.chinese: 她用剪刀剪纸(她用剪刀剪紙)。
examples.english: She uses scissors to cut paper.

examples.chinese: 妈妈帮我剪头发(媽媽幫我剪頭髮)。
examples.english: Mom helps me cut my hair.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢剪小说中的插图(他喜歡剪小說中的插圖)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān jiǎn xiǎoshuō zhōng de chātú.
examples.english: He likes to cut illustrations from novels.
